audit_status.source_base: Greek Romans text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for strong bearing weak, Messiah serving circumcision, nations praise, Paul's mission, collection for Jerusalem, travel plans.

verses.restored: We who are strong ought to bear the weaknesses of the not-strong and not please ourselves.
Let each of us please the neighbor into the good, toward building up.
For Messiah also did not please himself, but just as it is written: The insults of those insulting you fell upon me.
For whatever was written beforehand was written for our instruction, so that through endurance and through encouragement of the Scriptures we might have hope.
May the Divine of endurance and encouragement give you to think the same among one another according to Messiah Jesus,
so that with one accord, with one mouth, you may glorify the Divine and Father of our Lord Jesus Messiah.
Therefore receive one another, just as Messiah also received you, into glory of the Divine.
For I say Messiah has become servant of circumcision on behalf of truth of the Divine, to confirm the promises of the fathers,
and for the nations to glorify the Divine for mercy, just as it is written: Because of this I will confess you among nations and sing to your name.
And again he says: Rejoice, nations, with his people.
And again: Praise the Lord, all nations, and let all peoples praise him.
And again Isaiah says: There will be the root of Jesse, and the one rising to rule nations; upon him nations will hope.
May the Divine of hope fill you with all joy and peace in trusting, so that you may abound in hope in power of holy Spirit.
I myself am persuaded about you, my siblings, that you yourselves are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.
But I wrote to you more boldly in part, as reminding you, because of the grace given to me by the Divine,
to be a public servant of Messiah Jesus to the nations, priestly-serving the good news of the Divine, so that the offering of the nations may become acceptable, sanctified in holy Spirit.
Therefore I have boasting in Messiah Jesus toward the Divine.
For I will not dare to speak of anything except what Messiah worked through me into obedience of nations, by word and work,
in power of signs and wonders, in power of Spirit, so that from Jerusalem and in a circle as far as Illyricum I have fulfilled the good news of Messiah.
And thus being ambitious to announce good news not where Messiah was named, so that I might not build on another's foundation,
but as it is written: Those to whom it was not announced concerning him will see, and those who have not heard will understand.
Because of this I was also hindered many times from coming to you.
But now, having no more place in these regions, and having had longing for many years to come to you,
whenever I go to Spain, I hope while passing through to see you and by you to be sent forward there, if first I may be filled in part with your company.
But now I am going to Jerusalem, serving the holy ones.
For Macedonia and Achaia were pleased to make a sharing for the poor among the holy ones in Jerusalem.
For they were pleased, and they are debtors to them; for if the nations shared in their spiritual things, they ought also to serve them in fleshly/material things.
Therefore, completing this and sealing to them this fruit, I will depart by way of you to Spain.
I know that when I come to you, I will come in fullness of Messiah's blessing.
I urge you, siblings, through our Lord Jesus Messiah and through the love of the Spirit, to struggle together with me in prayers to the Divine on my behalf,
so that I may be rescued from the disobedient in Judea, and that my service to Jerusalem may become acceptable to the holy ones,
so that coming to you in joy through the will of the Divine, I may rest together with you.
May the Divine of peace be with all of you. Amen.

translation_notes: Romans 15 continues the weak/strong conscience theme by calling the strong to bear the not-strong and to receive one another.
Paul's nations mission remains tethered to Jerusalem through material sharing for poor holy ones; this keeps the Jewish/nations relationship reciprocal, not replacement-based.
